Intense heatwave and thunderstorm alert grips Telangana; Scattered rains forecast
Telangana is experiencing severe heatwave conditions, with Adilabad hitting 44.3°C. The IMD has issued alerts for Hyderabad and nearby districts, forecasting continued high temperatures despite chances of isolated thunderstorms and gusty winds in some areas
Hyderabad: Amid the intense summer heat, Adilabad sizzled at 44.3 degree Celsius topping the high temperatures list of Telangana on Wednesday.
Day temperatures remained high across most parts of the state with Nizamabad recording 44.2 degree Celsius, Medak logging in 42.6 degree Celsius, Khammam with 42.4 degree Celsius and and Mahabubnagar recording 41.9 degree Celsius.
The grueling heatwave continues to dominate with maximum temperatures soaring past 44 degree celsius in northern districts and 40 degree Celsius in Hyderabad.
While the India Meteorological Department (IMD), Hyderabad, has forecast isolated thunderstorms, the mercury is expected to remain high in several regions.
Hyderabad remains under orange alert as temperatures hover above 40 degree Celsius. In the last 24 hours, Hayathnagar emerged as the hottest region at 41 degree Celsius followed by Hakimpet (40.8) Rajendra Nagar (40.5), Begumpet (40.4) and Patancheru (39.4).
The IMD, Hyderabad forecast indicated that isolated places in Hyderabad, Medchal-Malkajgiri, Rangareddy, and Sangareddy are likely to experience thunderstorms and gusty winds on Thursday evening or early hours of Friday.
Despite the potential for localized rain, the IMD warns that there will be no large change in maximum temperatures for the next three days. Looking ahead to May 3, the heatwave conditions are projected to intensify further, with temperatures in Hyderabad and surrounding districts expected to range between 41 degree Celsius and 44 degree Celsius.

title: Telangana continues to reel under heatwave - The New Indian Express

full_text:
HYDERABAD: Even as the state reels under a heatwave for the third consecutive day, the India Meteorological Department, Hyderabad, on Wednesday forecast thunderstorms, lightning, strong winds and possible hailstorms across parts of the state over the next three days.
On Wednesday, the highest temperature was recorded in Adilabad at 46°C, followed by Kollur (Kamareddy) at 45.9°C, Dhanora (Komarambheem), Bhainsa (Nirmal), Allipur (Jagtial), Mosra (Nizamabad) recording 45.8°C and Kamanpur (Peddapalli) at 45.4°C. In Hyderabad, the highest was recorded in Osmania University at 42.4°C.
According to observations, a trough formed from an earlier upper air cyclonic circulation has now extended from Rayalaseema to the Comorin region, passing through south interior Karnataka and Tamil Nadu at an altitude of about 0.9 km above sea level.
Meanwhile, a previously active upper air cyclonic circulation over north Telangana and adjoining areas has weakened.
The weather department has predicted that light to moderate rainfall is likely at isolated places over the next three days. Maximum temperatures across the state are expected to remain largely unchanged during this period.
